description: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.

subcriteria.name: vulnerability_scanning
subcriteria.reference_text: Internal and external vulnerability scans performed quarterly or continuously. Nessus, Qualys, Tenable, or equivalent scanner used. Scan reports show identified vulnerabilities with CVSS scores. Remediation plan created for critical and high severity findings.

subcriteria.name: patch_management
subcriteria.reference_text: Patch management process ensuring security patches applied within defined SLAs. Critical patches applied within 48 hours, high within 30 days. Patch compliance dashboard showing percentage of systems patched. Monthly or weekly patch review by security team.

subcriteria.name: configuration_management
subcriteria.reference_text: Baseline configuration standards defined for servers, containers, and cloud resources. CIS benchmarks or hardening guides applied. Configuration drift detected through automated scanning. Infrastructure as code ensures consistent configuration.

subcriteria.name: change_detection
subcriteria.reference_text: File integrity monitoring or configuration monitoring tool alerting on unauthorized changes to production systems. OSSEC, Tripwire, or cloud-native tools detecting unexpected modifications to critical files and configurations.

mismatch_signals.reason: Uptime monitoring is not vulnerability or configuration management.
Access management is CC6.x, not vulnerability management.
